_about: M2 astrology reference vectors. Planetary sidereal longitudes computed via Swiss Ephemeris (pyswisseph 2.10.3, SIDM_LAHIRI). Vimshottari dasha section filled from drikpanchang.com. The ±0°05' and ±1° tolerance gates apply — SE and SwiftAA use the same Lahiri ayanamsa definition and VSOP87 planet tables so agreement should be well within tolerance.
source: pyswisseph 2.10.3 (SIDM_LAHIRI) for planets/lagna; drikpanchang.com (Drik Ganita, Lahiri) for dasha
retro_note: Rahu and Ketu are always retrograde by definition (mean node regresses). Sun and Moon are never retrograde. Other planets: speed < 0 at the given instant.
time_note: All times are UT (UTC). Lagna is location-sensitive — computed for lat/lon below at the given jd_utc.
lagna_source_note: lagna_sidereal_deg captured from Swiss Ephemeris swe.houses_ex() (independent house engine), NOT from the hand-rolled oblique-ascension formula — so the test reference is independent of the formula under test. Correct ascendant formula: atan2(cos θ, -(sin θ·cos ε + tan φ·sin ε)). Note: a sign error here yields the Descendant (exactly 180° off) and would pass a self-referential test — that is why these come from an independent source.

planetary._comment: 2024-08-26 05:30 UT — San Jose. Mercury retrograde (inferior conjunction window Aug 5-29). Saturn also retrograde. Overlaps golden_vectors Janmashtami case for Sun/Moon cross-check.
2024-12-21 05:30 UT — San Jose. Winter solstice. Mars retrograde + Jupiter retrograde — good stress test for two simultaneous retrogrades. Overlaps golden_vectors sanjose-2024-12-21.
2025-03-09 05:30 UT — San Jose. Venus retrograde. Overlaps golden_vectors sanjose-2025-03-09.

dasha._comment: Birth: 1986-02-20 08:03 IST, Vadodara (Gujarat, India). Mahadasha dates and antardasha boundaries captured from drikpanchang.com (Drik Ganita, Lahiri). KNOWN DISCREPANCY: drikpanchang's Lahiri ayanamsa constant differs from SE/SwiftAA SIDM_LAHIRI by ~0.142 degrees — producing ~5-day offsets in mahadasha starts and ~18-day offsets in antardasha starts vs. a SwiftAA-computed engine. Test gate is STRUCTURAL only: verify lord order, starting lord, sub-period durations match classical formula (±2 days per sub-period), and that exactly one mahadasha isCurrent. Do NOT gate on absolute mahadasha/antardasha dates vs. these drikpanchang values (the ayanamsa divergence makes ±1 day unachievable). See M2_PLAN.md §3.4.
dasha.balance_note: Not exposed in drikpanchang UI. Mahadasha start dates are the authoritative reference but carry ~5-day ayanamsa offset vs. SwiftAA — treat as informational, not as an absolute date gate.
